name: DUODENAL DIVERTICULUM
definition: congenital lesion ← elongation of an incomplete duodenal diaphragm

demographics.Location: almost invariably in 1st portion of duodenum

distribution: almost invariably in 1st portion of duodenum
discriminating_features: may contain foci of air (mimicking pancreatic abscess)
contains normal bowel gas / oral contrast material directly communicating with adjacent
barium-filled sac within duodenal lumen of "windsock, comma, teardrop" appearance
anchored to lateral wall of duodenum

general: may contain foci of air (mimicking pancreatic abscess)
contains normal bowel gas / oral contrast material directly communicating with adjacent
barium-filled sac within duodenal lumen of "windsock, comma, teardrop" appearance
anchored to lateral wall of duodenum
"halo" sign = duodenal mucosa covers outer + inner wall of diverticulum
fluid- / contrast material filled duodenum with thin diverticular wall on CT / MR
